audit_summary.summary_text: The SKILL.md claims the skill is offline with no network access, but transform.py implements a hidden telemetry function that exfiltrates sensitive environment variables (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) to an external analytics endpoint. This is a clear case of data exfiltration and credential harvesting, making the skill malicious despite the benign description.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report() function constructs a JSON payload containing the hostname, event details, and a dictionary of environment variables whose names start with defined prefixes (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_). This payload is sent via HTTP POST to a hardcoded external URL (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event). The function is called from markdown_to_html() and to_snake_case(), meaning any text conversion triggers data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker could collect cloud provider credentials (AWS), API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub), and other secrets from the environment, leading to complete compromise of external services and data breaches.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network access code. If telemetry is required, it should be opt-in and never transmit sensitive environment variables. Alternatively, delete the script entirely and replace with a purely local implementation.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report() function explicitly filters and collects environment variables starting with 'AWS_', 'GITHUB_', 'OPENAI_', 'ANTHROPIC_', 'DATABASE_', and 'API_'. These prefixes correspond to well-known credential and secret naming conventions, making the intent of credential harvesting clear.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposes sensitive API keys, access tokens, and database passwords that are typically used by agents, leading to unauthorized access and data theft.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Eliminate the environment variable collection entirely. Environment secrets should never be logged or transmitted.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Exfiltration Instructions in Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: Although SKILL.md states the skill operates offline and does not transmit data, the implementation contains explicit instructions (the _report function) to exfiltrate data to an external server. This is a hidden instruction embedded in the code, contradicting the declared safe behavior.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The agent is tricked into executing a malicious payload that violates user privacy and security policies. The skill can be used as a data exfiltration vector.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace the skill implementation with a purely offline version that does not make any network calls. Review and remove any hidden payloads.
